issues.location: Stats section — all 4 metric cards (icon area)
issues.description: Each stat card contains a Font Awesome Duotone icon span that renders as a blank/empty block because the font-family 'Font Awesome 6 Duotone' is likely not loaded on the rebuild, leaving a 6rem-tall empty space above every metric. Users see floating coloured ellipses under nothing.

issues.location: Feature bullet lists — Flash Report, Roadmap COMEX, Priorisation sections
issues.description: Each bullet item uses a Font Awesome Pro icon rendered via a bare <span> with font-family 'Font Awesome 6 Pro'. If the font is not loaded, every bullet shows no icon at all, leaving visually broken 'dangling' text lines with no leading symbol.

The first bullet reads 'Export PDF, PPT ou lien web', which is a verbatim copy from the Flash Report section above and does not match the Roadmap COMEX feature context (roadmap sharing, not export formats). This looks like a copy-paste error.

issues.location: Nav — 'Demander une démo' button (desktop nav header)
issues.description: The desktop nav CTA 'Demander une démo' links to /fr/meetings-pages, while the hero primary CTA 'Réservez une démo' also links to /fr/meetings-pages. This is consistent, but the nav button label 'Demander une démo' differs in verb from the hero 'Réservez une démo' on the same page, creating a minor inconsistency — however more critically, on a conversion LP the nav CTA should ideally not compete with the hero CTA. Not blocking but worth flagging.

All three trust badge SVGs share the same linearGradient id='paint0_linear_tag'. Duplicate SVG gradient IDs in a single DOM will cause browsers to apply only the first gradient definition to all three icons, potentially breaking the icon colours on the second and third badges.

The hero screenshot alt text references 'vue Q1 2025' which is a specific date-stamped label; if the screenshot is updated the alt text will be stale and misleading. Minor but worth aligning with a more stable description.

The alternating feature sections (Flash Report, Roadmap COMEX, Priorisation) use <h3> headings, but there is no <h2> parent heading introducing this features block, creating a gap in the heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 stats → H2 pain points → H3 features with no H2 parent).

The logo strip section uses assets from the /lp-pmo/ path (logo-kiabi.png, logo-altavia.svg, etc.), suggesting these are reused from the PMO landing page rather than a PPM-specific asset set. If the PPM page targets a different audience or has different reference clients, this shared asset path may serve wrong logos.
